THIS WEEK IN LOCAL GOVERNMENT

Marathon County learned it was denied FEMA public assistance after April's storms, while Kronenwetter and Weston boards moved forward on infrastructure spending and election preparations.

  • Marathon County was approved for FEMA individual assistance but denied public assistance due to insufficient documented damages; 24 county residents have already applied at disasterassistance.gov.
  • Kronenwetter's Village Board approved an amended bid of $814,815 for a six-bay equipment garage — up from the original design to add a second restroom — and approved ballot drop boxes for the August primary and November general elections.
  • Weston's Public Works Committee awarded the Concord Avenue and Bayberry Street reconstruction to HS Suns for approximately $2,248,819.90, and awarded a Howland Avenue surveying contract to Point of Beginning for $12,500.
  • Weston's Plan Commission unanimously approved a conditional use permit for a 1,090-square-foot detached accessory dwelling unit at 4804 Shore Avenue, prohibiting short-term rental, and forwarded approval of the 23-lot Bull Junior View subdivision plat to the Village Board.
  • Kronenwetter's Village Board directed staff to prepare a moratorium resolution on data centers and solar farms for the next board agenda, and to update the oak wilt enforcement ordinance.

Coming up: Monday brings a busy slate, including Weston's Board of Trustees at 6 p.m. and Kronenwetter's Plan Commission at 6 p.m., where commissioners are set to consider a zoning change on Marathon County Highway Department property to M2 General Industrial and a conditional use permit for a residential pond. Wausau's Plan Commission meets Tuesday at 5 p.m.

Full summaries on the Meeting Tracker →
